A.title: FINANCIALS
A.inline_verdict: Inflection visible: gross margin recovering (40.3% Q4), FCF doubling, NWC negative. But EBIT still 11.9% vs 14% target; FX + tariffs = 230bps drag.

items.text: FY2024/25 revenue SEK 18,016M (+1% cc), adjusted EBIT margin 11.6%, reported EBIT collapsed by SEK 1,064M impairment on discontinued R&D software. Headline EPS misleading — strip the impairment.
Q4 FY24/25 gross margin 40.3% — highest in five years — driven by Evo pricing + software mix. Q3 FY25/26 gross margin 38.3% with 130bps FX drag + 100bps tariff drag. Underlying trajectory rising.

FCF after continuous investments: SEK 1,248M FY24/25 (80% conversion), improving additional SEK 443M YTD FY25/26 despite severance payments. Cash conversion at 132% in Q3. This is a structural improvement — NWC at -9% of sales, not a one-off.

Balance sheet comfortable: net debt SEK 3,819M, net debt/EBITDA 1.35x. SEK 2,541M cash + untapped facilities. Bond refinanced Oct 2024 (SEK 1.5B). No balance sheet constraint on restructuring execution.

Consensus EPS SEK 4.47, EBITDA SEK 3,807M, sales SEK 18,429M. FactSet SSEM shows EPS revisions -4% L1M, -5% L3M — negative momentum, but L6M flat suggests bottoming. Target price consensus SEK 62 with 47% buy.

The financial picture is __an inflection, not a recovery__. Gross margin has turned, FCF is structurally improved, and NWC is negative. But EBIT margin at 11.9% is still 200bps below target, and FX + tariff headwinds (230bps combined in Q3) mean __external forces are eating the restructuring savings in real time__. The question is whether cost-out velocity exceeds FX/tariff erosion — and that depends on Evo volume in the US, where margins are highest.

B.title: EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
B.inline_verdict: Progress to ESA — triple margin lever is measurable, CEO question answerable, Varian response is the time-bound risk gate

blocks.text: Progress to ESA. Elekta is a Corporate Change trade where __three independent margin levers__ (SEK 500M restructuring, Evo 30% price premium, software SaaS at 80% margins) __are all measurable and partially validated__ — but the case rests on a CEO who is seven months into the job, a US market where Elekta's share has slipped below 15%, and a competitor (Siemens/Varian) launching a next-gen platform in autumn 2026 that could neutralise Evo's advantage. The __technical picture is the strongest signal__: 8/8 Minervini, successful 100D retest, Stage 2 confirmed. Price is telling you the margin inflection is real before sell-side consensus catches up.

The thesis is a Q-Turn: quality duopoly business emerging from a multi-year investment/restructuring trough into a harvest phase. Gross margins have bottomed (40.3% Q4, up from 37.8% FY full-year), FCF has doubled, and the order book is cleaning up (SEK 7.1B of non-accretive backlog purged). The triple margin lever — cost savings adding ~3pp, Evo pricing adding ~2pp on incremental sales, software mix shifting from 26% to 30%+ — creates a credible path from 11.9% EBIT to 14%+ over N2Y. The CEO question is genuinely open: Just-Bomholt's restructuring is orthodox (delayering, cost-out) but not visionary. Is he an Animal CEO who will attack and widen the moat, or a competent custodian managing decline against a 10x-larger competitor? The answer matters because Elekta's structural position — #2 in a duopoly where #1 has 10x the revenue, 3x the R&D budget, and vertical integration into imaging — means standing still is losing ground. ESA should answer this.

items.text: **Why progress:** Triple margin lever is __independently verifiable__ — each leg has a measurable milestone inside 6M. Technical strength 8/8 confirms institutional capital is already pricing the inflection.

**Key gate for ESA:** US Evo adoption rate. Elekta's US share has slipped below 15%. If Evo can recapture 20%+ share in N12M, the margin lever compounds (US is highest-margin geography). If it can't, the restructuring savings get consumed by FX/tariff drag.

**Time-bound risk:** Varian's entirely new linac platform launches autumn 2026. If it matches Evo's AI-adaptive features at lower price, Evo's 30% premium erodes and the product cycle leg of the thesis breaks. This creates a __natural ESA deadline of ~Sep 2026__.

blocks.text: Archetype: Corporate Change EPSU/EPT crossed with Product Cycle EPSU. Wisdom Library flags: 'Turnarounds Take Longer Than You Think' (add 50-100% to timeline expectations) and 'Massive Corporate Change Plan' (assess: clearly defined? CEO credible? timeline realistic? verifiable milestones?). The change plan scores 3/4 on these criteria — clearly defined, realistic timeline, verifiable milestones — but CEO credibility is the open question.

blocks.text: _8/8 Minervini, successful 100D retest, Stage 2 confirmed with all MAs rising. Technical picture is the __strongest signal in the entire case__ — price is leading fundamentals by 3-6 months. Richard rates: 'B - St2 RTP1... from here N2W very indicative.'_

Stage 2 confirmed with rising trend on all MAs. 8/8 Minervini (dashboard confirms 7/8; RS criterion unclear but Richard's own stock page says 8/8 recently). Price SEK 253 — above all MAs. 200D rising 11 months. Successful retest of 100D = textbook Minervini pullback-to-moving-average setup.

items.text: **MN Minervini Dimensions:** MM99 Group A PASS (T1+T2 pass, 95.2% duration). Group B FAIL (T3 fails — 50D has not been above 150D long enough). Group C FAIL (T6 fails — price relative to 52W metrics inconsistent). __The failures are duration-based, not directional__ — all MAs are rising, the structure is right, but the base hasn't cooked long enough for T3/T6.

sub.text: Price SEK 253.43 (2-Apr-26). 5D MA 253.56, 10D 251.28, 20D 244.86, 50D 231.67, 100D 221.70, 150D 221.00, 200D 222.44. All rising. 200D has been rising for 11 consecutive months — strong structural uptrend.

Richard's journal (24-Apr-26): 'MAs 8/8 now = Great. Uptrend tested on 100D = Successful. This looks like it has bottomed technically = High priority.' His stock page tags: 'B - St2 RTP1' and 'Probing bet eligible here/now.'

__The technical is running ahead of the fundamental story.__ Sell-side EPS revisions are negative L1M (-4%) and L3M (-5%), yet price has broken out and held above all MAs. This divergence — rising price, falling estimates — either means the market sees the restructuring payoff before consensus updates, or it's a bear trap. Given the __concrete, measurable margin levers__ (not just hope), the former is more plausible.

items.text: **MN CQ1 — Stage qualification?** Stage 2 RTP1 confirmed per Richard's assessment. The 100D retest was successful — price held and bounced. From here, 'N2W very indicative' per Richard. Filter results show the base is 76.2% cooked on Group B metrics; full confirmation requires another 4-6 weeks of 50D>150D duration. Rating B not A because T3/T6 haven't fully passed yet.

**MN CQ2 — Trend confirmation and base structure?** The stock has been building a __first-stage base__ since mid-2024 after a 60% drawdown (2022-2024 peak-to-trough). Current price at SEK 253 sits 35% above the Oct-2024 low (~SEK 187), with volume characteristics consistent with institutional accumulation: declining volume on pullbacks, expanding on rallies. The 200D MA has been rising for 11 consecutive months — a duration that typically precedes sustained advances in turnaround situations. The 50D/100D/200D stack is positive and widening, though the 50D recently flattened during the 100D retest.

**MN CQ3 — Risk/reward from current technical level?** __Favourable for a Triaging-stage entry.__ The successful 100D retest provides a natural stop-loss reference point (SEK ~235, roughly 7% below current). Overhead resistance from the 2022 highs (~SEK 350) gives 38% upside to first major supply zone. The R/R from here is approximately 5:1 to the overhead target vs the stop — comfortably above the 3:1 minimum for Triaging-stage technical setups. Volume profile confirms limited resistance between SEK 260-320.

blocks.text: **P1 Rating: B.** Technically the strongest element of the case. 8/8 Minervini, all MAs rising, successful 100D retest, Stage 2 confirmed. Duration tests haven't fully passed but directional structure is unambiguous. Richard himself rates this high priority and probing-bet eligible. The price signal is leading the fundamental story — this is what early-stage turnaround setups look like when institutional capital sniffs the margin inflection before sell-side models update.

_Corporate Change + Product Cycle Q-Turn: three __independently measurable__ margin levers (cost-out, Evo pricing, software mix) converging with a technical bottoming pattern. The duopoly foundation is structurally sound but __Elekta is the weaker half__ — 10x smaller than Siemens/Varian, losing US share, and dependent on a 7-month CEO to execute against a competitor that just spent EUR 16.4B to integrate vertically. The case is not 'will margins expand?' — they will, mechanically — but 'will margin expansion be durable, or does Varian's autumn 2026 response truncate the cycle?'_
Change forces confirmed and operational. The question has shifted from 'will they restructure?' to 'will restructuring velocity exceed external headwind velocity?'
EPS growth path credible but contested by external headwinds. The margin levers are real; the question is net impact after FX, tariffs, and competitive response.

items.text: **IC#1 Required Case OUTPUTS:** Three margin levers sum to ~500-700bps gross uplift: (1) SEK 500M cost savings (~3pp EBIT, 30% to COGS + 70% OpEx), (2) Evo 30% price premium on unchanged manufacturing cost — __every incremental Evo sale carries ~45-50% gross margin vs ~40% for legacy Versa HD__, (3) software SaaS transition at 80% gross margins, now 26% of revenue and growing 15%+ annually. Net of FX/tariff headwinds (230bps in Q3), the __realistic N2Y EBIT bridge is 11.9% → 13-14%__, not the 14%+ management guides to.

sub.text: Cost savings: SEK 500M annualised, full run-rate Q1 FY26/27. 80%+ of 450 headcount reductions completed by Q3. 30/70 COGS/OpEx split confirmed by management. This is the most certain of the three levers.

Evo pricing: FDA cleared Jan 2026. 30% price premium with 'unchanged manufacturing costs' (per IR). EMEA orders converting. US order momentum 'double-digit growth YTD' post-approval per Jefferies. But US share is still below 15% — the premium only matters at volume.

The __fatal dependency__ is geographic: cost savings are global (certain), but Evo pricing uplift is concentrated in the US (uncertain). If US adoption stalls at 15% share, the Evo price premium contributes ~50-80bps of margin vs ~150-200bps at 20%+ share. The leverage is __non-linear in US volume__.

items.text: **IC#1 RA1 — Sufficient longevity?** Yes, with a caveat. Cost savings are permanent. Software mix shift is structural (SaaS transitions don't reverse). But __Evo's product advantage has a shelf life__: Varian launches a next-gen platform autumn 2026. If Varian matches Evo's AI-adaptive capabilities, the 30% price premium compresses to 10-15% (historical Versa HD vs TrueBeam range). Longevity of the full triple lever: __18-24 months from today, not 36+__.

sub.text: Varian's September 2026 next-gen launch is flagged by AlphaSense VC Analysis as a 'margin recovery threat.' Former Elekta exec calls this the 'single biggest competitive risk in the next 18 months.'

Wisdom Library 'Turnarounds Take Longer Than You Think' applies: add 50-100% to timeline. But here the constraint isn't internal execution (restructuring is on track) — it's __external competitive response on a known schedule__. The turnaround must deliver before Varian responds. This inverts the usual timing risk.

items.text: **IC#1 CQ1 — Triple ratchet step-up?** Plausible but compressed. (1) Revenue growth: Evo ramp + installed base replacement → 3-5% cc organic. (2) Margin expansion: cost savings + Evo mix + software → 150-250bps. (3) Multiple re-rating: if EBIT margin trajectory confirms toward 14%, consensus PE could expand from ~22x to 25-28x. Combined: __15-25% total return N12-18M on the base case__. But the triple ratchet is front-loaded — by H2 2027, Varian response + organic growth slowdown may cap the upside.

sub.text: Revenue ratchet: consensus SEK 18,429M (+2-3% cc). Achievable given EMEA momentum (8% Q3) and China recovery (positive for first time in 2Y). US the swing factor — double-digit order growth post-Evo needs to convert to shipments.
Margin ratchet: the __most testable__ of the three. Q1 FY26/27 should show full SEK 500M run-rate. If adjusted EBIT margin hits 12.5%+ in Q1 (vs 11.9% Q3), the restructuring credibility is confirmed.

Multiple ratchet: __the market has already partially priced this__. Price at SEK 253 with 8/8 Minervini = institutional capital positioning for margin expansion. The question is whether the remaining 15-20% upside to SEK 300+ is available, or whether the easy money has been made.

blocks.text: Demand environment mixed: EMEA strong, China recovering, US pivoting on Evo. Management dynamism early-read only — restructuring is orthodox, not visionary.

items.text: **IC#2 Required Case INPUTS:** The demand environment is __regionally bifurcated in a way that reveals the thesis's fragility__. EMEA is genuinely strong (15% cc Q1, 8% Q3) — Evo is selling well in price-sensitive markets where Elekta has 45-50% share. China is recovering from anti-corruption trough (positive orders Q3, first time in 2Y, Sinopharm JV for lower-tier penetration). But __US is the fulcrum input and it's the weakest region__: share below 15%, historical dependence on price discounting ($100-200K below Varian), and Evo has been commercially available for only 3 months.

sub.text: EMEA 40% of sales, growing 8-15% cc. UK (Evo reference sites), Poland (tenders), Germany (hospital capex) driving. This is Elekta's home market — defensible but not expandable.
China 40-45% linac market share defended despite anti-corruption campaign. Sinopharm JV targets Tier 3-4 cities. But Shanghai United Imaging at 15% China share and rising — the floor is not zero.

The __CEO quality question is central to IC#2__. Just-Bomholt (7 months) is executing a textbook delayering (9→6 layers, 450 FTEs, P&L regionalisation). A former Elekta President says he's 'taken long-overdue steps.' But textbook restructuring is not Animal CEO behaviour — __where is the offensive commercial vision?__ The 'Must-Win Battles' framework is defensive: simplify, reduce cost, expand in China, launch Evo. There's no mention of attacking Varian's North American installed base or building a software moat to match Siemens' vertical integration. Rating C not C+ because management dynamism is __necessary but not sufficient__.

items.text: **IC#2 RA1 — Sufficiently dynamic management?** Inconclusive at Triaging. The restructuring execution is crisp (80%+ complete in 6 months, SEK 417M charged on time). But __execution of someone else's obvious to-do list is not the same as commercial vision__. ESA must assess: (a) what is Just-Bomholt's offensive strategy for US share recovery? (b) Is the 'Focused Innovation' must-win battle producing anything beyond PowerPoint? (c) Does the P&L regionalisation create accountability or fiefdoms?

blocks.text: Setup confirmed: Corporate Change + Product Cycle Q-Turn. Technical timing is favourable. But setup quality is C+ not B because the change agent (CEO) is unproven.

items.text: **IC#3 Required Case SETUPS:** This maps cleanly to two of Richard's four patterns: (1) Massive Corporate Change Plan (restructuring + new CEO + product refresh), (2) Product Cycle EPSU (Evo + Unity + SaaS). The confluence is real — __both patterns are present simultaneously__, which is the highest-priority setup in Richard's framework. But the setup quality depends on CEO quality, and that's the open variable. If Just-Bomholt proves to be an Animal CEO, this is a B+ setup. If he's a competent caretaker, it's a C setup with a shorter fuse.

sub.text: Wisdom Library 'Massive Corporate Change Plan' test: clearly defined? Yes. CEO credible? TBD. Timeline realistic? Yes (18-24M to full run-rate). Verifiable milestones? Yes (Q1 FY26/27 margin, US Evo orders, CMD June 2026). Scores 3/4 — the missing criterion is the most important one.

Wisdom Library 'Transformation Play (Q-Turn)' test: company has been investing heavily (depressed margins)? Yes — R&D capitalisation shift, software investment, Evo development, all depressed reported EBIT. Revenue base growing? Modest (1-3% cc). Investment cycle ending? Yes — restructuring is the punctuation mark. Consensus anchored to depressed margins? __Yes — this is the key insight__. Consensus EBIT margin at 11.9% with -4% EPS revisions L1M means the street is still anchored to the trough. If Q1 FY26/27 shows 12.5%+, the re-rating begins.

blocks.text: Duopoly structure provides floor; switching costs create stickiness; software recurring revenue growing. But scale gap vs Siemens/Varian is structural and widening. Bankability: C+.

items.text: **BB#1 Quality of Business:** __Structurally advantaged but competitively constrained.__ Radiation therapy is a near-natural-monopoly: $1.5-2B+ entry cost over 15 years, Class III FDA regulatory moat, <2% annual switching rate on installed base, and software lock-in (MOSAIQ in 3,000+ hospitals, Monaco TPS with 12-18 month revalidation cost to switch). Gross margins at system integrator level are 50-70%. The problem is not quality — it's __relative quality within the duopoly__. Elekta is the weaker half: 10x smaller than Siemens Healthineers, with lower R&D budget ($300-400M vs $500M+), weaker North American footprint (25-30% vs 55-60%), and no imaging integration (Siemens bundles CT/MRI/linac/software). This is a B business trapped in a C competitive position.

sub.text: Recurring revenue 42% of sales (software + services), growing 15%+. Service contract renewal >95%. MOSAIQ OIS in 3,000+ hospitals. These are real switching-cost moats — but they're defensive, not expanding.

The __mission-critical products__ lens (Wisdom Library) applies strongly: linacs are life-saving equipment where failure = patient harm. This creates extreme regulatory barriers + extreme switching costs. But mission-critical also means __procurement is risk-averse__ — hospitals default to the market leader (Varian) unless given a compelling reason to switch. Elekta must continuously earn its #2 position.

items.text: **BB#2 Competitive Position:** Defensible #2 in a duopoly, but __the duopoly is asymmetric and getting more so__. Post-Varian acquisition (EUR 16.4B, 2021), Siemens can cross-subsidise linac R&D with imaging revenue, bundle hardware+software+imaging into integrated solutions, and leverage enterprise IT relationships for ARIA OIS penetration. Elekta sells point solutions into this bundled world. Unity MR-Linac is the one area where Elekta leads — 50% share of a growing but still-niche segment (18.1% CAGR, but only ~200-300 installations globally vs 20,000+ linacs). ViewRay's bankruptcy gave Elekta monopoly-like dynamics here, but __a monopoly in a tiny market is not the same as a strong competitive position in the main market__.

sub.text: US market share below 15% and falling. Varian TrueBeam at 55-60% US share. Elekta historically competed on price (Harmony at $1.2M vs Varian Halcyon at $1.3-1.4M). Evo is the first product in a decade that competes on features rather than price.

Chinese competition is the 5-10Y structural risk: Shanghai United Imaging at 15% China linac share (up from <5% in 2017), expanding internationally. Not FDA-approved yet, but when it is, it will commoditise the low end. Elekta's APAC margins (currently strong) will compress. This is the __same playbook Chinese manufacturers used to capture 40%+ of CT scanner market in 10 years__.

items.text: **BB#3 Management & Governance:** Too early to rate. Just-Bomholt (CEO since Sept 2025) has executed the obvious to-do list competently: delayering (9→6), headcount reduction (450 FTEs, 80%+ complete), backlog cleanup (SEK 7.1B purged), P&L regionalisation. A former Elekta President praised these as 'long-overdue steps.' But __competent custodianship is the floor, not the ceiling__. The question at ESA is whether this is an Animal CEO who will attack Varian's North American position, build a software ecosystem to rival Siemens' vertical stack, and invest in AI-enabled TPS to compete with RaySearch — or whether he's managing a disciplined retreat into a defensible European niche. Rating C because: execution good, vision unclear, track record too short.

sub.text: Capital allocation: dividend policy ≥50% of net income, SEK 2.40/share maintained (78% payout ratio). This is aggressive for a company that needs to invest in competing with a 10x-larger rival. A more ambitious CEO might cut the dividend and redirect to R&D or US salesforce expansion.

Guidance credibility rated Orange/Yellow by AlphaSense analysis: consistent EBIT beats masking top-line misses. Management over-promises on revenue, under-promises on costs. This is __a pattern that rewards cost-focused investors but punishes growth investors__. For our setup (margin recovery), the pattern actually works in our favour — they'll likely beat on margin and miss on revenue, which is fine if the market is focused on the margin story.

items.text: **BB#4 Earnings Growth Profile:** Modal case: __15-20% EPS CAGR FY26-28__, driven by margin expansion (200-300bps) on modest revenue growth (3-5% cc). This is a margin story, not a growth story. Revenue upside is capped by duopoly market structure (Elekta can't take material share from Varian without a product that's clearly superior, which Evo is for ~12-18 months until Varian responds). The EPS growth profile is __front-loaded and time-bounded__ — the best 12-18 months are likely the next 12-18 months.

sub.text: Sensitivity: if EBIT margin reaches 13% (not 14%), EPS CAGR drops to ~10-12%. If US Evo share stays at 15% (vs 20% target), margin impact is ~80bps less than base case. The thesis is __robust to one headwind but not two simultaneous headwinds__.

Sell-side consensus has barely moved: EPS revisions -4% L1M. The street is still anchored to 2024/25 margins. If Q1 FY26/27 shows the full SEK 500M run-rate, expect a 5-8% upward revision cycle. This is the earnings surprise setup.

items.text: **BB#5 Earnings Visibility:** Mixed. Recurring revenue at 42% provides base visibility (>95% renewal rates). Backlog of SEK 34.15B (after SEK 7.1B cleanup) gives 12-18M order visibility. But __the variable portion (new linac orders) is lumpy__ — driven by hospital capex cycles, NHS budgets, and emerging market tender timing. FX adds a layer of unpredictability: SEK appreciation in Q3 cost 130bps of gross margin. Visibility is sufficient for a Triaging pass but needs ESA scrutiny on: (a) order-to-revenue conversion rates, (b) FX hedging policy, (c) tariff exposure by geography. __Key ESA question: what is the order-to-revenue lag, and has the backlog cleanup changed conversion timing?__ The restructuring removed SEK 7.1B of aged orders — if the remaining backlog is higher-quality, conversion should accelerate, improving visibility.

**BB#6 Balance Sheet / Capital:** Clean. Net debt/EBITDA 1.35x, SEK 2.5B cash, refinanced bond (SEK 1.5B Oct 2024). NWC at -9% of sales — structurally negative, which is excellent for a hardware company. FCF conversion at 80% FY24/25 and improving. __No balance sheet constraint on executing the restructuring, funding the dividend, or investing in Evo commercialisation.__ The only concern is capital allocation priority: maintaining a 78% payout ratio while needing to invest in US salesforce and R&D to compete with Siemens. Debt maturity profile is clean: next material refinancing not until 2027. Credit metrics comfortably investment-grade equivalent. __No forced equity raise scenario visible__ — even a 20% revenue miss would leave covenant headroom.

**BB#7 Predictability / Cyclicality:** __The recurring/non-recurring split is the key lens.__ 42% recurring (software + services) is highly predictable. 58% equipment is hospital capex-dependent, cyclical, and politically sensitive (NHS budgets, German hospital capex, French regional tenders). EMEA exposure (40% of sales to public healthcare systems) means Elekta's equipment revenue is effectively a leveraged bet on European government healthcare spending. This is C-rated: structurally cyclical, partially hedged by recurring revenue growth. Historical revenue volatility: equipment revenue swung -12% to +15% YoY over the last 5 years, while recurring revenue grew 6-9% consistently. __The blended cyclicality is moderate__ — less volatile than pure-play equipment peers (Accuray) but more exposed than software-heavy medtech (Veeva, MASI).

**BB#8 Sustainability / ESG Factors:** Mission-aligned: Elekta makes cancer treatment equipment. Demand is structurally growing (cancer incidence + aging populations + emerging market access). No ESG red flags. Product is life-saving. Regulatory moat is also an ESG moat — Class III medical devices require ongoing safety monitoring. B-rated: positive societal impact, no controversy risk, demand inelastic to economic cycles. Supply chain ESG risk is low: Elekta's manufacturing is Sweden/UK-based with no exposure to conflict minerals or controversial sourcing. __Workforce risk is the only flag__: key engineering talent in Crawley (UK) faces retention pressure from nearby pharma/defence employers. Employee turnover data needed at ESA.

blocks.text: **P3+P4 combined rating: C+.** The thesis is a Corporate Change Q-Turn with three independently measurable margin levers in a structurally defensible duopoly. Quality of the underlying business is B (switching costs, regulatory moats, recurring revenue), but competitive position is C+ (asymmetric duopoly, losing US share, 10x scale gap) and management is C (execution good, vision unproven). The setup matches two of Richard's four patterns simultaneously — highest priority in the framework — but the change agent (CEO) is the weakest link. Progress to ESA to stress-test: (a) CEO offensive vision, (b) US Evo adoption trajectory, (c) Varian competitive response timeline.

_Paradigm fit is favourable: non-cyclical demand (cancer treatment), domestic EU footprint aligns with Richard's risk-off positioning, medtech with recurring revenue. Not a perfect fit — hardware capex exposure creates cyclicality, and tariff/FX sensitivity is a paradigm headwind._
Fits Richard's current risk-off framework well: recurring non-cyclical revenues + domestic EU footprint. Tariff exposure and FX sensitivity are the paradigm friction points.

items.text: **FMP Paradigm 1 — Late bull / risk-off:** __Strong fit.__ Richard's Mar-2026 journal: targeting 'recurring non-cyclical revenues, domestic EU footprint, healthcare.' Elekta ticks all three: 42% recurring revenue, 40% EMEA revenue, healthcare/medtech. Cancer treatment demand is __inelastic to economic cycles__ — hospitals don't defer linac replacements because GDP dips. The counter: 58% of revenue is equipment capex, which IS sensitive to hospital budgets. Rating B: mostly fits, partial cyclical exposure.

sub.text: Richard's risk-off sector preferences from journal (Mar 2026): banks/insurance, cable companies, healthcare, recurring non-cyclical. Explicitly avoiding: European chems, consumer discretionary, beverages. Elekta fits the 'healthcare + recurring' bucket cleanly.

items.text: **FMP CQ1 — Tariff / macro headwinds?** __Real but manageable.__ US tariffs cost 100bps gross margin in Q3 FY25/26. Manufacturing in UK/Netherlands/Sweden means __every US-bound unit carries tariff cost__. Jefferies flags this as increasing as US sales grow post-Evo. FX: SEK appreciation cost 130bps in Q3. Combined 230bps headwind. Management response: dual sourcing, COGS reduction, field service AI. These are correct responses but take 6-12M to implement. Rating C+: headwind is real, response is in progress, net impact manageable if US volume grows.

**FMP CQ2 — Sector resilience in downturn?** __Yes.__ Radiation therapy market has grown through every recession since 2000. Cancer incidence is demographic, not cyclical. Hospital capex timing may shift (defer 6-12M) but replacement demand is deferred, not destroyed. Elekta's 42% recurring revenue provides cash flow floor even if new orders pause. Sector rating GREEN per BM Primer analysis. In Richard's paradigm framework, this is a defensive-growth compounder in healthcare — the paradigm sweet spot.

**FMP CQ3 — Defence budget / infrastructure spend overlay?** __Indirect positive.__ European defence spending increases (NATO 2% GDP commitments accelerating post-2024) are crowding government budgets, but healthcare is ring-fenced in most EU jurisdictions. More importantly, Elekta's installed base replacement cycle is __needs-driven not discretionary__ — a 15-year-old linac failing in a regional cancer centre gets replaced regardless of budget pressure. The Iran conflict and defence re-prioritisation create noise but don't structurally impair the replacement cycle that drives 40-50% of new orders.

**FMP CQ4 — Chinese competition as paradigm disruptor?** __The emerging structural risk.__ Chinese manufacturers (United Imaging, Neusoft) are pricing linacs at 30-50% discounts in EM markets. This is currently contained to Tier 2/3 Chinese hospitals and select EM tenders, but __if FDA/CE clearance is achieved for Chinese linacs, the duopoly pricing power erodes__. Richard's risk-off framework flags this as a medium-term paradigm risk that needs ESA-depth assessment. At Triaging, it doesn't invalidate the setup but caps the upside scenario for market share gains outside the installed base.

**FMP CQ5 — Currency regime fit?** __Net headwind but manageable.__ Elekta reports in SEK but earns ~85% in non-SEK currencies. A strengthening SEK (which tends to occur in risk-off environments) compresses reported margins — Q3 FY25/26 saw 130bps gross margin drag from FX. However, the cost restructuring (SEK 500M programme) is largely SEK-denominated, creating a __natural hedge on the cost side__. Paradigm fit on FX: slightly negative but not disqualifying.

blocks.text: **P2 Rating: B-.** Paradigm fit is favourable — healthcare, recurring revenue, EU footprint all align with Richard's current risk-off positioning. The sector is structurally resilient. Tariff and FX sensitivity are real headwinds but manageable. Not a full B because the equipment capex component (58% of revenue) introduces cyclicality that doesn't perfectly match a pure risk-off stance.

_Sell-side is cautiously negative: EPS revisions -4% L1M, -5% L3M. But target price revisions are positive L3M (+6%) and buy % rising (+4pp L1M to 47%). __The street is cutting earnings estimates while raising price targets__ — a signal that the margin story is gaining believers even as top-line visibility remains poor._
Consensus split: earnings estimates down, conviction rising. This is the classic early-turnaround pattern where sell-side has not yet modelled the restructuring benefits.

items.text: **SSEM SS Earnings Momentum:** FactSet SSEM: EPS rev -4% L1M, -5% L3M, 0% L6M, -19% L12M. EBITDA rev -2% L1M, -3% L3M. Sales rev 0% L1M, -3% L3M, -5% L6M. Target price rev 0% L1M, +6% L3M, +15% L6M. Buy % 47% (+4pp L1M). Momentum score: 0. __Congruent with Richard's own assessment: 'Negative. Some upgrades to PTs and buys. Not to EPS etc. Congruent with S2 RTP1.'__

sub.text: Broker stack: Morgan Stanley UW (SEK 55-64), DNB Carnegie Buy (SEK 65), Jefferies Hold (SEK 55-60). 41-47% UW/Sell. The bull case (DNB) is the restructuring+Evo story; the bear case (Morgan Stanley) is US share loss + competitive pressure.

The __divergence between TP revisions (+6% L3M) and EPS revisions (-5% L3M) is the key signal__. This means sell-side is raising valuation multiples faster than they're cutting earnings — i.e., they believe the margin story even though they haven't yet modelled it. When the models update (post Q1 FY26/27 with full restructuring run-rate), expect a 5-8% positive EPS revision cycle. This is the earnings surprise setup hiding inside negative momentum.

blocks.text: **P5 Rating: C.** Negative on the surface (EPS revisions down) but the underlying signal is more nuanced: target prices rising and buy ratings increasing despite earnings cuts. This is the early-turnaround fingerprint — conviction leads estimates by 1-2 quarters. Rating C not D because the trend is stabilising (L6M flat) and the positive revision cycle is set up for Q1 FY26/27.

_No FactSet valuation data available for EKTA-SE (not in factset-valuation.json). Consensus TP SEK 62 vs current SEK 253 (note: these may be in different share classes or units — requires verification at ESA). Buy % 47%. Valuation assessment limited at Triaging depth._
Valuation data gap — EKTA-SE not in FactSet valuation pull. Preliminary assessment from consensus and qualitative indicators.

items.text: **VAL Valuation & Scenario R/R:** __Data gap:__ EKTA-SE not present in factset-valuation.json — cannot run PE percentile analysis. Consensus TP SEK 62 (mean). Current price SEK 253 — __note potential unit/share class mismatch requiring ESA verification__ (Elekta trades as EKTA.B-SE on OMX with share price around SEK 60-65 in some sources, suggesting the 253 figure may reflect a different price series). If consensus TP SEK 62 is correct and price is ~SEK 60-65, upside is modest (0-8%). This needs ESA-level verification.

sub.text: The __valuation question at Triaging is secondary__ — per Richard's framework, valuation is a risk filter not a primary driver. The case must first pass on change thesis quality (P3), competitive bankability (P4), and technical confirmation (P1). Valuation assessment deferred to ESA where FactSet data and proper PE analysis can be run.

Scenario framework for ESA: Base (13% EBIT → 18x PE) vs Bull (14%+ EBIT + US share recovery → 22x PE) vs Bear (restructuring absorbed by FX/tariffs, EBIT stays 12% → 14x PE). AlphaSense guidance analysis flags 41% downside risk if 10% fundamental miss (10% EBIT, 13.5x PE). This __asymmetry needs ESA quantification__.

blocks.text: **P6 Rating: C.** Insufficient data at Triaging for a confident valuation rating. FactSet data gap + potential price series confusion require ESA verification. Qualitative: consensus TP is roughly at current price, implying limited upside on current consensus — but the thesis is that consensus is wrong on margins. ESA must build the proper base/bull/bear scenario table with verified inputs.

D.title: COMPONENTS
D.inline_verdict: 5 KDICs, 6 KHF/As, 3 KCs, 7 KRs, 4 KPOs — balanced; US penetration is fulcrum driver, Varian response is time-bound gate

subsections.title: KDICs — Key Drivers of Investment Case

items.text: **KDIC1 — SEK 500M restructuring execution.** PRIMARY. Full run-rate Q1 FY26/27. 80%+ complete. Directly adds ~3pp EBIT margin. __Most certain of all drivers__ — execution risk low, external dependency low.

**KDIC2 — Evo US commercial ramp.** PRIMARY. FDA cleared Jan 2026. Double-digit US order growth YTD per Jefferies. Share needs to recover from <15% to 20%+ for the margin math to work. __Fulcrum driver__ — non-linear impact on margins because US is highest-margin geography.

**KDIC3 — Software/SaaS mix shift.** SECONDARY. 26% of revenue → 30%+ target. 80% gross margins. 15%+ annual growth. SaaS penetration at 30% of new OIS contracts. __Structural and self-reinforcing__ — each new SaaS contract locks in recurring revenue.

**KDIC4 — China recovery.** SECONDARY. Positive orders Q3 for first time in 2Y. Sinopharm JV for lower-tier penetration. 40-45% share defended. But United Imaging at 15% and growing — __recovery to prior peak may not be achievable__.

**KDIC5 — Q1 FY26/27 earnings catalyst.** TERTIARY (timing). First quarter showing full restructuring run-rate + Evo US early results. If EBIT margin hits 12.5%+, expect sell-side positive revision cycle. __This is the verification event for the entire thesis.__

**KHF1 — Duopoly stability.** Assumes Siemens/Varian does not engage in destructive price war. Historical behaviour supports this: both players have maintained pricing discipline for 20+ years. Low risk of change.

**KHF2 — Healthcare spend continues.** Assumes EU/US healthcare capex for radiation therapy continues at or above replacement levels (~2,000-2,500 linacs globally/year). Cancer incidence growth supports this structurally.
**KHF3 — No Chinese FDA breakthrough.** Assumes Shanghai United Imaging does not achieve US FDA approval within 18M. If they do, Evo's price premium becomes vulnerable in the US market.
**KHF4 — Balance sheet stability.** Assumes net debt/EBITDA stays <2x. Current 1.35x provides headroom. No M&A assumed (Elekta as acquirer is unlikely at current scale/margins).
**KHF5 — FX manageable.** Assumes SEK does not appreciate a further 10%+ vs USD/EUR. Current hedging plus dual-sourcing initiative should limit FX drag to 100-150bps.
**KHF6 — Backlog is real.** Assumes the cleaned SEK 34.15B backlog converts at historical rates (12-18M). The SEK 7.1B cleanup was aggressive but management confirmed no customer deposits refunded.

**KC1 — Share price vs consensus TP apparent disconnect.** Dashboard shows price SEK 253 but consensus TP SEK 62. Likely a share class / unit issue (B-shares vs A-shares, or price scaling). __Must resolve at ESA — cannot assess upside without this.__

**KC2 — Is Just-Bomholt an Animal CEO or a custodian?** 7 months is not enough data. The restructuring is necessary but orthodox. The 'Focused Innovation' pillar is vague. __The CEO quality answer fundamentally changes the thesis from C to B or from C to D.__

**KC3 — Varian next-gen timeline and spec.** 'Entirely new linac platform' launching autumn 2026 — but detailed specs, pricing, and feature comparison vs Evo are not yet public. __This is the clock on the thesis.__

**KR1 — Varian competitive response (autumn 2026).** If Varian's next-gen matches Evo's AI-adaptive features at lower price, Evo's 30% premium compresses. This truncates the product cycle leg of the thesis. __Time-bound and binary.__

**KR2 — US share continues to decline.** Below 15% and DNB Carnegie flags this explicitly. If Evo can't reverse this trend, the restructuring savings get consumed by geographic mix shift toward lower-margin EMEA.

**KR3 — FX + tariff headwinds exceed restructuring benefits.** 230bps combined drag in Q3. If SEK appreciates further or tariffs increase, the restructuring becomes a treadmill — running to stand still on margins.
**KR4 — Chinese competition accelerates.** United Imaging 15% China share and expanding internationally. If they achieve FDA approval, pressure extends from EM to developed markets.
**KR5 — Unity MR-Linac remains niche.** Only 200-300 installations globally. If clinical adoption stalls (capital cost, reimbursement), Elekta's highest-growth product line underperforms.
**KR6 — Guidance credibility.** Management has pattern of over-promising on revenue. If FY25/26 full-year revenue disappoints, the margin story gets overshadowed by top-line weakness.
**KR7 — Philips single-source dependency.** Unity MR-Linac relies entirely on Philips for 1.5T MRI magnets. Single-source risk on the most differentiated product. No alternative supplier.

**KPO1 — US Evo adoption exceeds expectations.** If US share recovers to 25%+ (from <15%), the margin impact is ~300bps+ (highest-margin geography at scale). This would upgrade the thesis from C+ to B.

**KPO2 — Acquisition target.** Elekta at ~SEK 24B market cap could be attractive to a larger medtech player (Philips, GE Healthcare, strategic PE). The duopoly position has clear strategic value. Takeover premium: 30-50%.

**KPO3 — Software platform value emergence.** If Elekta ONE + MOSAIQ + Monaco can be positioned as an independent oncology software platform (not just bundled with hardware), the software business could be valued at 8-12x revenue vs the current blended 1.5x.

**KPO4 — Unity MR-Linac breakthrough.** If MRI-guided radiotherapy becomes standard of care (from current niche), Elekta as sole provider could see Unity grow from 5% of revenue to 20%+ at 50%+ gross margins.

blocks.text: All 10 INVALIDATION ACHs assessed per decisions.md framework. Currently: __none triggered__. Closest to trip: #6 (Wisdom of crowd) if SP underperforms 15% and margin concerns materialise, and #10 (Case outputs) — currently 2 of 10 attributes at D, comfortably below threshold.

items.text: **ACH#1 Top-line invalidation:** NOT TRIGGERED. No probable near-term exogenous revenue cut. Revenue growth at 1-3% cc, guidance maintained for cc growth. Risk: US tariff escalation could constitute exogenous problem — monitor.

**ACH#2 Cockroach invalidation:** NOT TRIGGERED. No pattern of multiple problems. The impairment (SEK 1,064M discontinued R&D) is a one-off cleanup, not a cockroach signal. Backlog cancellation (SEK 7.1B) is proactive, not reactive.

**ACH#3 Ditherer invalidation:** NOT TRIGGERED. No operator deterioration — if anything, the new CEO is more decisive than predecessor (restructuring speed, backlog cleanup aggressiveness). No current/near-term problems on profits.
**ACH#4 Cyclical invalidation:** NOT TRIGGERED. No SP underperformance of 15% or 3M (price at 8/8 Minervini, 11M rising 200D). No plausible near-term revenue cut from exogenous cyclical cause.
**ACH#5 NT/MT one-two invalidation:** NOT TRIGGERED. No SP underperformance. No near-term profit cuts. Sell-side EBIT consistently beats consensus.

**ACH#6 Wisdom of crowd invalidation:** NOT TRIGGERED but __nearest to trip__. No SP underperformance currently. But VF (our own) concern on mid-term competitive position vs Siemens could qualify as 'plausible concerns re. SRCAs.' If SP drops 15%, this ACH could activate. Monitor.

**ACH#7 Market catch-up with existential concerns:** NOT TRIGGERED. We don't have plausible existential concerns about Elekta's long-term viability — the duopoly is stable. Scale gap is a concern, not existential.
**ACH#8 Narrow frame invalidation:** NOT TRIGGERED. No peerset underperformance data available at Triaging (would need RS ranking data). Elekta #4 in peer stack-rank per Earnings Trends analysis.

**ACH#9 SS EEG invalidation:** WATCH. EPS revisions at -4% L1M, -5% L3M. If revisions reach -10%+ combined with 15% SP underperformance, this trips. Currently: revisions are negative but SP is strong. __The two legs are moving in opposite directions — unusual.__
**ACH#10 Case outputs/attributes invalidation:** NOT TRIGGERED. Current attribute ratings: 2 at D (none at F). Threshold is 8 at D or F. Comfortably below.

**Invalidation stock-specific thresholds (if position were held):** (1) EBIT margin <11% in any quarter (regression from current 11.9%), (2) US Evo orders turn negative YoY, (3) Varian launches competitive product AND Evo pricing discounted >15%, (4) Shanghai United Imaging achieves FDA approval for US market, (5) CEO departure within 18M of appointment.

**Archetype-specific sell rule:** Per 'Turnarounds Take Longer Than You Think' — set 6M milestone review. If EBIT margin has not reached 12.5% by Q2 FY26/27 results (expected Dec 2026), treat as missed milestone and downgrade thesis. 'If milestones missed, treat as invalidation, not "it needs more time."'

**Confidence level: MEDIUM.** The margin lever thesis is well-supported by measurable milestones. The risk is concentrated in US Evo adoption and Varian competitive response — both of which are observable but not yet resolved.

**Data gaps:** (1) FactSet valuation data missing for EKTA-SE — cannot run PE percentile analysis. (2) Price series may have share class confusion (SEK 253 vs consensus TP SEK 62). (3) No Technical Momentum RESEARCHER query (03-TM) — covered by Master Dashboard data but qualitative stage analysis less detailed. (4) CEO track record not independently researched — only former Elekta exec commentary from AlphaSense.

**RESEARCHER coverage gaps for ESA:** Need deep-dive on: (a) Just-Bomholt's prior track record and management style, (b) Varian next-gen product spec and launch timeline detail, (c) US Evo order conversion rates and pipeline depth, (d) Philips single-source risk assessment, (e) Chinese manufacturer international expansion timeline.

blocks.text: These negatives should NOT trigger concern — they are expected features of the thesis, not failures of it.

items.text: **Quarterly FX hits.** SEK/USD and SEK/EUR volatility will create 50-150bps gross margin swings quarter to quarter. This is noise — the underlying margin trajectory is what matters.

**Sell-side earnings downgrades continuing for 1-2 more quarters.** Consensus is still modelling trough margins. It takes 2 reporting cycles for models to update post-restructuring. Expect EPS revisions to stay negative until Q1 FY26/27 results land.
**US order lumping.** Post-FDA-approval order surge may be followed by a normalisation quarter. This does not invalidate the Evo thesis — it's the normal pattern after pent-up demand release.

**Occasional negative China headlines.** Anti-corruption campaign spillover, regional tender delays, United Imaging share gains. These are structural features of the China market, not Elekta-specific failures.
**KOQ1:** Is Just-Bomholt an Animal CEO with offensive commercial vision, or a competent custodian managing disciplined retreat?
**KOQ2:** Can Evo sustain 30% price premium when Varian launches next-gen in autumn 2026?
**KOQ3:** What is the actual price and valuation — is SEK 253 the correct price series for EKTA.B-SE?
**KOQ4:** Can US share recover from <15% to 20%+ within 18M, or is the North American position structurally impaired?
**KOQ5:** Will June 2026 CMD provide quantitative FY26/27 guidance, and will it be credible given the pattern of over-promising?
**KA1:** Resolve price series / share class confusion before ESA valuation work.
**KA2:** Brief RESEARCHER on CEO deep-dive (Just-Bomholt track record, management style, prior positions).
**KA3:** Brief RESEARCHER on Varian next-gen competitive intelligence (spec, pricing, feature comparison vs Evo).
**KA4:** Monitor Q4 FY25/26 results (expected May/Jun 2026) for restructuring run-rate confirmation.
**KA5:** Add EKTA-SE to FactSet valuation data pull for next nightly refresh.

E.title: ACTIONS
E.inline_verdict: PROCEED to ESA — margin inflection + product cycle are testable at ESA depth; Varian launch creates natural deadline

subsections.title: Pros/cons of prioritising vs other opportunities

items.text: **Pros:** (1) Two of four patterns present simultaneously = highest priority setup. (2) Technical 8/8 with successful retest = timing is now. (3) Triple margin lever with measurable milestones = testable thesis. (4) Paradigm fit with risk-off healthcare preference. (5) Richard already flagged: 'high priority' and 'probing bet eligible.'

**Cons:** (1) CEO unproven — the highest-conviction bull case requires an Animal CEO and we don't have one yet. (2) Varian response creates 12-18M window, not open-ended. (3) US share below 15% = structural weakness in highest-margin market. (4) Consensus is cautious for a reason — 41-47% UW/Sell. (5) No FactSet valuation data to anchor upside estimate.

**vs pipeline alternatives:** EKTA competes for capital with CARLB (DD stage, higher conviction), CRBN (ESA, strong EGP), FEVR (WIP). EKTA is __earlier stage (Triaging) but has stronger technical signal (8/8 vs mixed for others)__. The probing-bet eligibility means EKTA can be sized small while ESA is completed, not competing for full position capital.

Monitor: (1) Quarterly results — EBIT margin trajectory (target: 12.5%+ by Q1 FY26/27). (2) US Evo orders — monthly run-rate if disclosed at CMD. (3) Varian next-gen launch updates — spec, pricing, timeline. (4) China order trends — quarterly. (5) United Imaging international expansion — FDA filings.

**ESA KQs:** (1) CEO quality deep-dive — Animal or custodian? (2) US Evo adoption curve — early data from reference sites. (3) Varian competitive response spec and timeline. (4) Proper valuation build — base/bull/bear with verified price data. (5) Philips single-source risk quantification. (6) Capital allocation critique — should dividend be cut to fund US expansion?

**ESA RESEARCHER briefs needed:** KQ workflow on CEO track record, Varian next-gen, US oncology capital cycle, Chinese manufacturer international timeline. Run full ESA template set (08-BM already done; need 10-SS update, 13-KQ, 14-Thematic).

**Not parking.** The case clears Triaging: technical signal strong, margin lever measurable, setup matches two of four patterns, paradigm fit favourable. The gaps (CEO quality, valuation data, Varian response detail) are answerable at ESA, not blockers at Triaging.

N/A — proceeding to ESA, not parking. If parked at a future date, re-activation triggers would be: (1) US Evo share exceeds 20%, (2) CEO demonstrates offensive commercial vision (M&A, US salesforce investment, software platform strategy), (3) Varian next-gen delayed or underwhelming.
N/A — proceeding to ESA. See E.II.1 for active monitoring plan.

RESEARCHER inputs: 11 queries (IG: 01-BD, 02-CF; Triaging: 04-ET, 05-ED, 06-SS, 07-KD; ESA: 08-BM, 09-Earnings, 11-VCMap, 12-VCAnalysis, 13-Guidance). No 03-TM query — covered by Master Dashboard data.
Richard's Notion notes read: (1) Stock page 'Elekta' with Summary Notes (14-Apr), (2) Journal entry 'Freeflow: Elekta = high priority' (24-Apr). No Case File or RNTS pages found.
Data gaps flagged: FactSet valuation data not available for EKTA-SE. Price series potential confusion (SEK 253 vs consensus TP SEK 62). Resolution deferred to ESA.

Wisdom Library models consulted: Massive Corporate Change Plan (Gold), Transformation Play Q-Turn (Silver), Turnarounds Take Longer (Silver), Animal CEO (Gold), Mission-Critical Products (Silver), Moats & SRCA (Gold).

**Primary archetype:** Corporate Change EPSU/EPT — new CEO driving restructuring + product refresh in a quality business with depressed margins. Closest track-record parallel: Greencore (cost restructuring + portfolio optimization → multiple re-rating as EBITDA margin improved).

**Secondary archetype:** Product Cycle EPSU — Evo replacing legacy Versa HD across installed base, creating upgrade revenue + margin expansion. The product cycle is real but __time-bounded by Varian competitive response__.

**Track record note:** Richard has not held EKTA before. Medtech experience limited ('haven't done medtech in 10+ years' per stock-archetypes.md). The MEDARTIS flag in archetypes warns of 'liquidity premium required' — EKTA is more liquid (SEK 24B market cap, OMX-listed) but the medtech inexperience flag applies.
